resume_data.raw_text:
HARSHITH VASANTH SHETTY
7795471769 harshithshetty779@gmail.com github.com/homieshetty /in/homie-shetty/
SUMMARY
Seeking a challenging role in the field of coding, where I can leverage my programming skills and
dedication to excel in software development.
TECHNICAL SKILLS
Python | HTML | CSS | SQL | C
Tools : Git , Github , Vercel , Netlify , Firebase (Auth,Firestore) , Supabase
PROJECTS
Smart Finance & Workforce Tracker Feb 2025 - Current
Built a role-based workforce and finance platform with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and Firebase,
including event tracking, automated wage updates, dashboards, CSV/PDF reporting, real-time data,
chat, and task management in a dark-themed responsive interface.
TechSpark - Technical Event Management Platform Sept 2025 - Current
Developed a full-stack event management platform using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase,
and Framer Motion, enabling real-time participant registration with validation and verification, and
providing an admin dashboard with live tracking, search, exports, and role-based access control.
Planical: AI-Powered Mental Health Support Platform October 2024
Built a mental health platform with React.js, FastAPI, LangChain, Groq API, Firebase, ChromaDB, and
Socket.io, offering an AI chatbot, real-time doctor-client chat, wellness features, multi-language
dashboard, user analytics, and scalable, secure architecture for 1000+ users.
RPS-AI: Intelligent Rock-Paper-Scissors Game March - April 2025
Created an AI Rock-Paper-Scissors game in Python using OpenCV, MediaPipe, and CustomTkinter, with
real-time hand gesture recognition, Markov chain–based AI, interactive GUI, stats tracking, and multi-
threaded smooth gameplay.
EDUCATION
Final year, B.E. in Computer Science and Engineering 2022-2026
Shree Devi Institute of Technology
CERTIFICATIONS AND ACHIEVEMENTS
Code Meet 2024 Hackathon: Participated in a 24-hour hackathon at Srinivas University Institute of
Engineering & Technology, gaining hands-on experience in problem solving and teamwork.
(October 2024)
React.Js and Javascript ChatGPT clone by Devtown(issued in 2024)
Bootcamp on Backend development using NODEJSand MongoDB By Devtown(issued in 2024)
Frontend web development using React.Js and Javascript by Devtown (issued in 2024)
Participated in Sankalp’25 held at Shree Devi Institute of Technology (issued in 2024)
Participated in Sankalp’24 held at Shree Devi Institute of Technology (issued in 2025)
Created a website for the college technical event TechSpark, enabling smooth event promotion and
participant engagement.
Achieved 2nd place at Technical quiz at Srinivas University.
EXPERIENCE
Experienced in efficiently managing groups of up to 80 people for event coordination, including roles
at Ocean Pearl.
Volunteered for a Harry Potter–themed event at Paper Rocket, Mangalore.
Handled logistics and transport operations for a large-scale event.
Managed and coordinated teams across multiple events under Moulding Dreams.
Created a website for
red_flags: Many skills listed but very few projects found. This may reduce credibility.

questions.question: What is the most important principle in Full Stack Developer and why?
questions.why_prompt: Give a real example from your work or projects.
options: Writing clean, maintainable, and well-documented code that others can understand
Optimizing performance at every layer, even before functionality is complete
Shipping features as fast as possible to meet business deadlines
Using the newest and most popular frameworks regardless of project needs

questions.question: How do you approach debugging a complex issue in production?
questions.why_prompt: Walk me through a real incident you dealt with.
options: Reproduce locally, check logs, isolate variables systematically, then fix and monitor
Restart the service and hope the issue resolves itself
Ask a colleague to look at it since debugging is time-consuming
Roll back the last deployment without investigating the root cause

questions.question: How would you design a scalable API for Google's Full Stack use case?
questions.why_prompt: What tradeoffs did you consider in your design?
options: Use load balancing, caching (Redis), database sharding, and async queues for scale
Build a monolith first, then split into microservices only when traffic demands it
Use a single server with vertical scaling since it is simpler to manage
Rely entirely on third-party SaaS solutions to avoid infrastructure complexity

questions.question: What practices do you follow to ensure high code quality?
questions.why_prompt: How have these practices helped your team in the past?
options: Code reviews, unit/integration tests, linting, CI pipelines, and documentation
Manual testing only since automated tests slow down development velocity
Having the senior dev review all code before merging without tests
Skipping reviews for small changes since they rarely introduce bugs

questions.question: How would you design a scalable API for Amaxon's Full stack use case?
questions.why_prompt: What tradeoffs did you consider in your design?
options: Use load balancing, caching (Redis), database sharding, and async queues for scale
Build a monolith first, then split into microservices only when traffic demands it
Use a single server with vertical scaling since it is simpler to manage
Rely entirely on third-party SaaS solutions to avoid infrastructure complexity

questions.question: What practices do you follow to ensure high code quality?
questions.why_prompt: How have these practices helped your team in the past?
options: Code reviews, unit/integration tests, linting, CI pipelines, and documentation
Manual testing only since automated tests slow down development velocity
Having the senior dev review all code before merging without tests
Skipping reviews for small changes since they rarely introduce bugs

latest_result.verdict: The candidate demonstrates severe weaknesses in core full-stack development areas, especially in problem-solving, production debugging, system design for scalability, and modern code quality practices. The complete lack of meaningful reasoning for any answer further indicates a significant gap in understanding and articulation.

gaps.why_weak: The candidate proposes an unsystematic and ineffective approach ('restart the service and hope the issue resolves itself') for debugging complex production issues. This demonstrates a critical lack of understanding of systematic problem-solving, incident management, and root cause analysis in a production environment.

resources.name: Systematic Debugging Strategies in Software Engineering

gaps.why_weak: While starting with a monolith can be a valid architectural choice, the answer for a 'Hard' system design question fails to address the specific mechanisms and considerations for *scalability* (e.g., caching, load balancing, asynchronous processing, database scaling, stateless services, API gateways), indicating a superficial understanding of designing high-traffic systems beyond just architectural style.

gaps.skill: Modern Code Quality & Automated Testing Practices
gaps.why_weak: The candidate explicitly dismisses automated testing as slowing down development velocity and relies solely on manual testing. This is contrary to industry best practices and reveals a fundamental misunderstanding of how to achieve high code quality, prevent regressions, and ensure long-term development efficiency and velocity.

resources.name: Benefits of Automated Testing in Software Development
Martin Fowler - The Practical Test Pyramid

question_review.question: What is the most important principle in Full Stack Developer and why?
question_review.candidate_answer: Writing clean, maintainable, and well-documented code that others can understand
question_review.correct_answer: The most important principle in full-stack development is often considered to be 'creating maintainable and scalable solutions.' This encompasses clean code, good documentation, robust architecture, and efficient problem-solving, ensuring the system can evolve and be supported over its lifecycle by multiple developers.
question_review.what_was_wrong: While 'Writing clean, maintainable, and well-documented code' is an excellent core principle, the candidate's reasoning 'lvk' is completely nonsensical and does not demonstrate any understanding or ability to articulate why this principle is important or how it's applied.

resources.name: Clean Code Principles for Full Stack Developers

question_review.question: How do you approach debugging a complex issue in production?
question_review.candidate_answer: Restart the service and hope the issue resolves itself
question_review.correct_answer: Debugging a complex production issue requires a systematic approach: start by gathering information (logs, metrics, monitoring tools), try to reproduce the issue in a non-production environment, isolate the problematic component, form hypotheses, test them, and finally implement and verify a fix. Continuous monitoring should be in place to prevent recurrence.
question_review.what_was_wrong: Restarting a service and 'hoping' for resolution is not a debugging approach; it's a workaround that avoids identifying the root cause and can often lead to recurring issues or production instability. It shows a critical lack of systematic problem-solving skills and understanding of production incident management.

question_review.question: How would you design a scalable API for Amaxon's Full stack use case?
question_review.candidate_answer: Build a monolith first, then split into microservices only when traffic demands it
question_review.correct_answer: Designing a scalable API for a platform like Amaxon involves several key strategies: implementing caching (CDN, in-memory, distributed), using load balancers, employing asynchronous processing for non-critical tasks (message queues), designing stateless services, optimizing database queries and schema, leveraging CDNs for static assets, and considering distributed systems principles for resilience and fault tolerance.
question_review.what_was_wrong: While starting with a monolith can be a valid architectural choice, the question asks *how* to design a *scalable* API. The candidate's answer only suggests an architectural style and completely omits crucial scaling mechanisms like caching, load balancing, asynchronous processing, database optimizations, or handling high traffic. The reasoning 'nl' is meaningless, further indicating a lack of detailed understanding.

question_review.question: What practices do you follow to ensure high code quality?
question_review.candidate_answer: Manual testing only since automated tests slow down development velocity
question_review.correct_answer: Ensuring high code quality involves multiple practices: comprehensive automated testing (unit, integration, E2E), code reviews to catch issues and share knowledge, continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines for automated checks, linting and static analysis tools, clear documentation, and adhering to coding standards and best practices.
question_review.what_was_wrong: Relying *only* on manual testing for code quality is highly inefficient and prone to errors, especially for a full-stack role with complex systems. Automated tests are critical for ensuring quality, catching regressions early, and *accelerating* long-term development velocity by enabling safe refactoring and faster deployments. Stating they 'slow down development velocity' reveals a fundamental misunderstanding of modern software development practices.

resources.name: Google Testing Blog: The Testing Trophy

next_attempt_tips: Develop a systematic approach to problem-solving and debugging, particularly for production environments.
Deepen understanding of core system design principles, focusing on concrete patterns and technologies for scalability, reliability, and performance.
Embrace and understand the critical role of automated testing, code reviews, and CI/CD in modern software development for achieving high code quality and velocity.
Practice articulating reasoning clearly and concisely to demonstrate understanding beyond just selecting an answer, as the provided reasoning was meaningless.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of React's Virtual DOM?
questions.why_prompt: Can you explain how this contributes to React's performance characteristics, and perhaps relate it to a time you optimized UI rendering in a project?
options: A. To directly manipulate the browser's native DOM for faster rendering.
B. To provide a JavaScript object representation of the UI, allowing React to efficiently calculate and apply UI updates.
C. To pre-render React components on the server for improved SEO and initial load times.
D. To manage component state and props, ensuring data immutability within the application.

questions.question: You are building a new serverless API endpoint using AWS Lambda and DynamoDB for a high-traffic e-commerce application. This endpoint needs to retrieve product details by `productId` and also support searching products efficiently by `category`. What is the most efficient DynamoDB design strategy for this scenario?
questions.why_prompt: Explain why your chosen strategy is superior to the others, specifically addressing scalability and cost considerations for both access patterns.
options: A. Use a single table with `productId` as the Partition Key and `category` as a Sort Key, relying solely on the primary key for all lookups.
B. Create a Global Secondary Index (GSI) with `category` as the Partition Key and `productId` as the Sort Key, while keeping `productId` as the primary Partition Key for the base table.
C. Implement two separate DynamoDB tables: one for `products_by_id` and another for `products_by_category`, managing consistency manually.
D. Store all product data in an S3 bucket and use AWS Athena for querying product details.

questions.question: You are designing a new feature for an online payment system that processes orders and updates inventory. The order processing involves two distinct microservices: `OrderService` (which records the order) and `InventoryService` (which decrements stock). To ensure data consistency across these services in a distributed environment, which pattern would you primarily recommend, and why?
questions.why_prompt: Discuss the trade-offs of your chosen approach, particularly concerning latency, complexity, and how it handles different types of failures (e.g., `InventoryService` failure after `OrderService` success).
options: A. Two-Phase Commit (2PC) protocol to ensure atomic updates across both services.
B. Use a Saga pattern with asynchronous messaging (e.g., SQS/SNS or Kafka) to orchestrate local transactions and compensate for failures.
C. Rely on eventual consistency by simply calling `InventoryService` after `OrderService` completes, assuming retries will handle transient failures.
D. Consolidate both services into a single monolithic service to ensure ACID transactions with a shared database.

questions.question: You notice that a complex React component with many child components is re-rendering excessively, even when its props or state haven't relevantly changed, leading to noticeable UI sluggishness. What is the most effective approach to optimize this specific component's rendering performance?
questions.why_prompt: Describe a scenario where memoization might not be the best solution, and what alternative strategies you might consider in such cases.
options: A. Refactor the component to use `useState` and `useEffect` hooks instead of class components and `shouldComponentUpdate`.
B. Implement `React.memo` for functional components or `shouldComponentUpdate` for class components, performing a shallow comparison of props and state.
C. Move all component state into a global Redux store to centralize data management.
D. Increase the `webpack` bundle size limit to allow for more JavaScript to be loaded initially.

latest_result.verdict: The candidate demonstrates good foundational knowledge in React concepts and excellent practical understanding of DynamoDB design for specific access patterns. However, there's a critical gap in distributed systems knowledge, particularly regarding appropriate transaction patterns for microservices. There's also a recurring pattern of selecting suboptimal or incorrect multiple-choice answers despite providing better reasoning, suggesting a need for more precise articulation or careful option evaluation.

skill_scores.feedback: Shows strong understanding of React's core mechanisms like the Virtual DOM and excellent knowledge of performance optimization techniques (memoization, profiling), though sometimes struggles with specific MCQ option choices.
Exceptional understanding of DynamoDB design principles for common access patterns, including single-table design and Global Secondary Index (GSI) usage for scalability.

Lacks fundamental understanding of appropriate distributed transaction patterns for microservices, specifically the pitfalls of Two-Phase Commit and the benefits of patterns like Saga for eventual consistency.

strengths: Solid understanding of DynamoDB schema design for efficient data access patterns.
Strong grasp of React performance optimization techniques (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback, profiling).
Good conceptual understanding of the Virtual DOM's purpose.

gaps.why_weak: Selected Two-Phase Commit (2PC) as the primary pattern for ensuring data consistency across microservices, which is an anti-pattern due to its blocking nature, performance overhead, and availability issues in large-scale distributed systems. The candidate did not demonstrate understanding of more suitable patterns like the Saga pattern for eventual consistency in distributed microservices.

resources.name: Saga distributed transaction pattern - Azure Architecture Center

gaps.skill: React Virtual DOM Nuances and Reconciliation
gaps.why_weak: While the candidate's reasoning demonstrated a general understanding of the Virtual DOM's purpose to optimize UI updates by minimizing direct DOM manipulation, the chosen MCQ option contradicted this by stating the VDOM 'directly manipulates' the browser DOM. This indicates a need for more precise understanding of the VDOM's intermediary role and the reconciliation process.

resources.name: Understanding the Virtual DOM in React

question_review.question: What is the primary purpose of React's Virtual DOM?
question_review.candidate_answer: A. To directly manipulate the browser's native DOM for faster rendering.
question_review.correct_answer: The primary purpose of React's Virtual DOM (VDOM) is to provide an in-memory representation of the actual DOM. React uses a diffing algorithm to compare the VDOM with a previous snapshot, calculating the minimal set of changes needed to update the browser's native DOM efficiently, thus *minimizing direct manipulation* for performance.
question_review.what_was_wrong: The candidate incorrectly chose an option stating the VDOM 'directly manipulates' the DOM. The Virtual DOM acts as an intermediary; React compares VDOM trees and then *React* (not the VDOM itself) performs the minimal updates on the *real* browser DOM. The candidate's reasoning, however, correctly identifies the goal of 'minimizing direct manipulation' which contradicts their chosen MCQ.

question_review.question: You are building a new serverless API endpoint using AWS Lambda and DynamoDB for a high-traffic e-commerce application. This endpoint needs to retrieve product details by `productId` and also support searching products efficiently by `category`. What is the most efficient DynamoDB design strategy for this scenario?
question_review.candidate_answer: B. Create a Global Secondary Index (GSI) with `category` as the Partition Key and `productId` as the Sort Key, while keeping `productId` as the primary Partition Key for the base table.
question_review.correct_answer: For efficient retrieval by `productId`, the base table should use `productId` as its primary Partition Key. To efficiently search by `category` and potentially sort within a category, a Global Secondary Index (GSI) is ideal, using `category` as its Partition Key and `productId` as its Sort Key. This design optimally supports both required access patterns.

question_review.question: You are designing a new feature for an online payment system that processes orders and updates inventory. The order processing involves two distinct microservices: `OrderService` (which records the order) and `InventoryService` (which decrements stock). To ensure data consistency across these services in a distributed environment, which pattern would you primarily recommend, and why?
question_review.candidate_answer: A. Two-Phase Commit (2PC) protocol to ensure atomic updates across both services.
question_review.correct_answer: For data consistency across microservices in a distributed environment, the Saga pattern is primarily recommended. It manages distributed transactions as a sequence of local transactions, with each service updating its own data and publishing events, using compensating transactions to undo prior work if a step fails. This approach maintains high availability and scalability, unlike Two-Phase Commit which introduces blocking and tight coupling.
question_review.what_was_wrong: The candidate recommended Two-Phase Commit (2PC), which is generally considered an anti-pattern for distributed transactions in modern microservices architectures due to its blocking nature, high latency, and single point of failure. The reasoning provided ('ok') was completely insufficient, indicating a severe lack of understanding of this critical distributed systems concept.

resources.name: Saga pattern vs Two-Phase Commit for microservices

question_review.question: You notice that a complex React component with many child components is re-rendering excessively, even when its props or state haven't relevantly changed, leading to noticeable UI sluggishness. What is the most effective approach to optimize this specific component's rendering performance?
question_review.candidate_answer: A. Refactor the component to use `useState` and `useEffect` hooks instead of class components and `shouldComponentUpdate`.
question_review.correct_answer: The most effective approach is to apply memoization techniques. For functional components, this means wrapping components with `React.memo` and memoizing values/functions with `useMemo` and `useCallback`, respectively. This prevents unnecessary re-renders of components and their children if their props or dependencies haven't shallowly changed, often preceded by profiling to identify bottlenecks.
question_review.what_was_wrong: The candidate chose refactoring to hooks, which, while a modern practice, does not inherently solve excessive re-renders; `shouldComponentUpdate` (or its functional equivalent, `React.memo`) is specifically designed for this. However, the candidate's reasoning correctly identified `React.memo`, `useMemo`, and `useCallback` as the key solutions, along with profiling, showing a strong practical understanding despite the incorrect MCQ choice.

next_attempt_tips: Deepen understanding of distributed transaction patterns for microservices, focusing on eventual consistency and the Saga pattern over 2PC.
Practice interpreting and selecting the most precise answers in multiple-choice questions, even when the underlying concept is understood, to ensure consistency between knowledge and articulation.
Explore different strategies for handling eventual consistency and idempotency in distributed systems for robust backend design.
